title: Corneria nausoriensis
intro:

Corneria nausoriensis is used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Corneria nausoriensis include alkaloids, terpenoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The plant's alkaloids, in particular, have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Based on limited information, the most common herbal preparations made with Corneria nausoriensis include infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
